title: Add secret detection to prevent credential leakage in prompts and logs
source: Supply chain security best practices 2025 — secret scanning in AI pipelines
description: The system currently strips CLAUDECODE from the environment but has no mechanism to detect secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) that may appear in ticket descriptions, agent prompts, or log output. A ticket description containing a leaked credential would be passed directly to Claude and potentially logged in .cto/logs/ JSONL files in plaintext.
proposed_changes: 1. Add a detect_secrets(text: str) -> list[dict] function in security_utils.py with regex patterns for common secret formats: AWS keys (AKIA...), GitHub tokens (ghp_/gho_/ghs_), generic API keys (Bearer/sk-/pk_), base64-encoded credentials, and high-entropy strings resembling secrets. 2. Add a redact_secrets(text: str) -> str function that replaces detected secrets with [REDACTED-TYPE]. 3. In delegate.py, orchestrate.py, and meeseeks.py, run redact_secrets() on all content written to .cto/logs/ JSONL files. 4. In the prompt construction path, run detect_secrets() on ticket descriptions and user input; if secrets are found, warn and optionally redact before sending to Claude. 5. Add CTO_SECRET_SCAN_MODE env var: 'warn' (default) or 'redact' to control behavior.
